meridian_skill.description:
Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages. Ensures every fact has
an inline [Source: ...] citation matching the standard format. Extended in
v0.25.1: scans for broken tweet/post references that lack actual URLs and
resolves them via the host's X / Twitter API integration.
meridian_skill.name: citation-fixer

outline.text: Step 3: Search for the actual tweet

sections.heading: Contract
lines: This skill guarantees:
Every brain page is scanned for citation compliance
Missing citations are flagged with specific location
Malformed citations are fixed to match the standard format
**(v0.25.1)** Tweet / post references without URLs are resolved via
X API and patched with deterministic `https://x.com/<handle>/status/<id>`
links
Results reported with counts (scanned, fixed, remaining)

sections.heading: Phases
lines: use judgment to scan pages and repair their citations:
List pages and read each one, checking for inline [Source: ...] citations
Identify facts without a citation, citations missing a date or source type, and citations with the wrong format
Rewrite malformed citations to match the quality convention
Resolve tweet references via the X API integration
Report counts of pages scanned, citations found, issues fixed, and remaining gaps

sections.heading: Tweet resolution pipeline (v0.25.1 extension)
lines: For each broken tweet reference, follow this chain. The actual API call
goes through whatever X integration the host has configured (typical
shape: a recipe under `recipes/x-api/` with handle / search-all
endpoints)

sections.heading: Step 1: Identify broken references
lines: Scan the page for patterns that indicate tweet references without URLs:
Contains words like `tweeted`, `posted`, `said on X`, `RT`, `retweet`,
`X post`
Contains quoted text that looks like a tweet (short, punchy, often
starts with a quote)
Has `[Source: ... X/Twitter ...]` without an `x.com` URL
References engagement metrics (likes, impressions) without a link

sections.heading: Step 2: Extract searchable content
lines: From each broken reference, extract:
The **handle** (if mentioned: `@<username>`)
The **quoted text** (if available)
The **approximate date** (often present in surrounding timeline entries)

sections.heading: Step 3: Search for the actual tweet
lines: Use the host's X API integration. Query patterns:

codeblock:plain:IyBIYW5kbGUgKyBxdW90ZWQgdGV4dDoKZnJvbTo8aGFuZGxlPiAiPGV4YWN0IHF1b3RlIGZyYWdtZW50PiIKCiMgUXVvdGVkIHRleHQgb25seToKIjxleGFjdCBxdW90ZSBmcmFnbWVudD4iCgojIE9yaWdpbmFsIG9mIGEgcmV0d2VldDoKIjxleGFjdCBxdW90ZT4iIC1pczpyZXR3ZWV0

sections.heading: Step 4: Verify and extract metadata
lines: Once a candidate is found:
Confirm the text matches the quoted fragment
Pull the tweet id, author handle, engagement metrics (likes / RTs /
impressions)
Construct the URL: `https://x.com/<handle>/status/<tweet_id>`

sections.heading: Step 5: Patch the brain page
lines: Replace the broken citation with a proper one:
**Before:**
codeblock:plain:IjxxdW90ZSBmcmFnbWVudD4iIFtTb3VyY2U6IDxzb21lIGhhbmQtd2F2eSBhdHRyaWJ1dGlvbj5d
**After:**

codeblock:plain:IjxmdWxsIHZlcmlmaWVkIHF1b3RlPiIg4oCUIDxOPiBsaWtlcywgPE4+IFJUcywgPE4+IGltcHJlc3Npb25zCltTb3VyY2U6IFtYLzxoYW5kbGU+LCBZWVlZLU1NLUREXShodHRwczovL3guY29tLzxoYW5kbGU+L3N0YXR1cy88dHdlZXRfaWQ+KV0=

sections.heading: Batch mode
lines: When sweeping many pages:

sections.heading: Find candidate pages
lines: codeblock:bash:

sections.heading: Priority order
lines: Recently created / updated pages — fresh broken refs are easiest to
resolve while context is fresh
High-traffic pages (frequent reads / writes from other skills)
Everything else — bulk cleanup over time

sections.heading: Rate limiting
lines: X API: respect the host's tier limits; don't hammer
Target ~50 pages per batch run
1-3 API calls per page (search + verify)
Batch-commit every 10-20 pages so a partial failure doesn't lose
progress

sections.heading: Output format
lines: codeblock:plain:Q2l0YXRpb24gQXVkaXQgUmVwb3J0Cj09PT09PT09PT09PT09PT09PT09PQpQYWdlcyBzY2FubmVkOiAgICAgICAgTgpDaXRhdGlvbnMgZm91bmQ6ICAgICAgTgpJc3N1ZXMgZml4ZWQ6ICAgICAgICAgTgpUd2VldCBsaW5rcyByZXNvbHZlZDogTgpSZW1haW5pbmcgZ2FwczogICAgICAgTiAocGFnZXMgd2l0aCB1bmNpdGFibGUgZmFjdHMp

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: ❌ Inventing citations for facts that have no source. Flag them
❌ Removing facts that lack citations (flag them; don't delete)
❌ Fixing citations without reading the full page context
❌ Batch-fixing without checking quality on a sample first
(see `conventions/test-before-bulk.md`)
❌ Composing tweet URLs by guessing the tweet id. Always go through
the X API; deterministic links only

sections.heading: Integration
lines: This skill can be called:
**Manually** — "fix citations on this page"
**As a batch cron** — weekly sweep of pages with broken refs
**By other skills** — `enrich` or `media-ingest` can call citation-fixer
before commit to validate output

sections.heading: Metrics
lines: If running as a recurring batch, track state in a small JSON file under
`~/.gbrain/citation-fixer-state.json`:

codeblock:json:ewogICJsYXN0X3J1biI6ICIyMDI2LTA0LTE1VC4uLiIsCiAgInBhZ2VzX3NjYW5uZWQiOiAwLAogICJjaXRhdGlvbnNfZml4ZWQiOiAwLAogICJ0d2VldF9saW5rc19yZXNvbHZlZCI6IDAsCiAgImNpdGF0aW9uc191bnJlc29sdmFibGUiOiAwLAogICJwYWdlc19yZW1haW5pbmciOiAxNDI0Cn0=

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: The skill's output shape is documented inline in the body sections above (see "Output", "Brain page format", or equivalent). The literal section header here exists for the conformance test (`test/skills-conformance.test.ts`)
